Section 03

Projects &
case studies

End-to-end BI projects spanning retail analytics and renewable energy — from raw data to decision-ready dashboards.

01
Power BI Retail DAX AI Analytics Star Schema
Retail chain sales & inventory performance
Mr. DIY–style retail chain · 4-dataset BI dashboard · Microsoft Power BI
+
Project objective

Design a Business Intelligence dashboard enabling a retail chain to monitor sales, inventory, customer, and marketing performance across multiple store locations — converting disconnected spreadsheet data into a single, decision-ready analytical system.

Business questions answered
Which products and categories generate the highest revenue and profit margins?
How do sales vary by location, time of day, and season?
Which stores are at risk of stockouts or overstocking?
How effective are marketing campaigns in driving sales?
What is the customer retention rate and average purchase value?
Datasets used
Sales data Date, store, category, revenue, profit
Inventory data Stock level, reorder point, stock status
Customer data Segment, loyalty, spend, frequency
Marketing data Campaign type, cost, ROI, conversions
DAX measures written
Total Revenue = SUM(Sales_Data[Total_Revenue])
Gross Profit Margin % = DIVIDE(SUM([Gross_Profit]), SUM([Total_Revenue]))
Average Order Value = DIVIDE([Total Revenue], COUNTROWS(Sales_Data))
Stock Turnover Rate = DIVIDE([Units Sold], AVERAGE([Stock_Level]))
Customer Retention Rate = DIVIDE([Returning Customers], [Total Customers])
Campaign ROI = DIVIDE([Revenue Generated] - [Campaign Cost], [Campaign Cost])
Key findings & insights
📈

Purchase frequency drives VIP status. Customers are 7.55× more likely to be classified as VIP when purchase frequency increases by 8.71 — the single strongest loyalty driver.

📦

Proactive inventory risk modelling. DAX IF logic classified every store-product combination as Low Stock, Optimal, or Overstock — enabling restocking before stockouts occurred.

🤖

AI-powered natural language queries. Q&A visual allowed non-technical managers to generate reports using plain English, significantly reducing time-to-insight.

Challenges & solutions
⚠ Inconsistent date formats across Excel sheets caused relationship errors in the data model.
✓ Used Power Query to standardise all dates to DD-MM-YYYY format before loading.
⚠ Many-to-many relationships between Sales and Customer tables caused ambiguous cross-filtering.
✓ Introduced a Customer_ID column to convert the key relationship to Many-to-One.
⚠ Map visual malfunctioned — could not display sales by store location.
✓ Enabled 'Use Map and Filled Map Visuals' under Power BI Security options and restarted.
Data architecture

A star schema was constructed in Power BI's model view with Sales_Data as the central fact table, connected to three dimension tables — Inventory, Marketing, and Customer — via shared key fields. The Customer_ID link enabled cross-analysis between transaction behaviour and customer demographics including loyalty membership, age group, and customer segment.

Dashboard screenshots

Sales overview — Total Revenue MYR 3.56M across 9 store locations, with monthly trend and category breakdown.

Inventory heatmap — colour-coded stock status (Low Stock / Optimal / Overstock) per store and category, with AI Q&A visual.

Customer segmentation — Key Influencers AI visual identifying purchase frequency as the primary driver of VIP classification.

Marketing analytics — Campaign ROI by type and monthly spend vs revenue generated, filterable by campaign type and date.

02
Power BI Solar Energy Project Management Pipeline Analytics i2 Energy
Solar PV project pipeline & performance intelligence
i2 Energy PMS · Real workplace data · Microsoft Power BI + Excel Gantt tracker
+
Project objective

Design a Business Intelligence system for a solar energy company to monitor the full lifecycle of solar PV installation projects — from initial proposal to client handover — enabling management to track pipeline value, project progress, PIC performance, and invoice milestone status across multiple clients and locations nationwide.

Business questions answered
What is the total pending vs acquired portfolio value across all PICs?
Which projects are stuck, on hold, active, or completed — and why?
How is each PIC (person-in-charge) performing across their project portfolio?
Which invoice milestones are outstanding across the project pipeline?
What percentage of each project's 15-stage construction process is complete?
Data & scope
Project pipeline Client, site, PIC, stage, status, progress %
Portfolio value Sum of pending & acquired per PIC (RM 27.2M total)
Invoice milestones Stage-based billing: arrival, authority, DC install, commissioning
Construction Gantt 15 stages from project lead to handover, per client & site
Key metrics tracked
⚡

RM 27.3M total portfolio — RM 15.7M pending, RM 11.6M acquired across 5 PICs managing projects for clients including PETRONAS, TNB Energy, MAHB, Nestle Malaysia, and MR DIY.

📊

52 projects tracked across 15 construction stages — 13 active, 14 completed, 12 on hold, 13 stuck — giving management real-time visibility into project health across all sites.

📅

Gantt-style progress tracker with target COD (Commercial Operation Date) and colour-coded stage completion — enabling proactive identification of delayed or stuck projects before they impact revenue.

BI tools & techniques applied
Sum of Pending = portfolio value not yet invoiced per PIC
Sum of Acquired = revenue already collected per milestone
Progress % = stages completed / total stages per project
Status classification = Active / Completed / On Hold / Stuck
Invoice stage filter = milestone-based billing pipeline view
Business impact

This dashboard replaced disconnected spreadsheet tracking with a single live BI view, enabling senior management at i2 Energy to instantly identify which projects are stuck, which PICs have the highest pending revenue exposure, and which invoice milestones are due — directly supporting faster collections and project delivery decisions.

Dashboard screenshots

i2 Energy PMS Power BI dashboard — pipeline value by PIC, project status distribution, invoice milestone breakdown, and portfolio donut chart showing RM 15.7M pending vs RM 11.5M acquired.

Excel-based Gantt tracker with 15 construction stages — colour-coded by completion status across 30+ active solar PV installation projects, with target COD dates and overall progress percentage per client site.

03
Microsoft Learn Power BI DAX Sales Analytics
Adventure Works sales analysis
Microsoft Learn · Power BI training module · Sample dataset
+
Project objective

Complete the Microsoft Learn Power BI training module using the Adventure Works sample dataset — a fictional global manufacturing and retail company — to develop proficiency in data modelling, DAX measure writing, and interactive report design within a structured, real-world business context.

Skills developed
Connecting and transforming data using Power Query within Power BI Desktop.
Building a relational data model with fact and dimension tables.
Writing DAX measures for year-to-date sales, growth rates, and targets.
Designing multi-page interactive reports with slicers, drill-throughs, and KPI cards.
Publishing reports to Power BI Service and configuring dashboard tiles.
Dataset overview
Sales data Orders, revenue, quantity by product & region
Product data Category, subcategory, cost, list price
Customer data Demographics, region, purchase history
Date table Calendar table for time-intelligence DAX
DAX measures written
Sales YTD = TOTALYTD([Total Sales], Dates[Date])
Sales vs Target = DIVIDE([Total Sales], [Sales Target]) - 1
Profit Margin % = DIVIDE([Total Profit], [Total Sales])
Prior Year Sales = CALCULATE([Total Sales], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR(Dates[Date]))
YoY Growth % = DIVIDE([Total Sales] - [Prior Year Sales], [Prior Year Sales])
Key learning outcomes
🎓

Time-intelligence mastery. Built YTD, prior year, and growth measures using DAX time functions — a critical skill for any real-world BI reporting environment.

🔗

Data model best practices. Learned the importance of a dedicated date table and proper one-to-many relationships for clean, unambiguous filtering across visuals.

🌐

Power BI Service deployment. Published the completed report to the cloud and configured dashboard tiles — simulating a real enterprise BI delivery workflow.

Source

Completed via Microsoft Learn — the official Microsoft training platform at learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/powerplatform/power-bi. The Adventure Works dataset is a widely recognised benchmark dataset used across Microsoft's BI and SQL Server training ecosystem.

04
Data Storytelling Customer Analytics AI Insights Strategic BI
What drives customer loyalty in retail?
Insight narrative · Retail chain data · Power BI Key Influencers AI visual
+
The business problem

Retail managers often know their sales numbers but rarely understand why certain customers become loyal VIPs while others remain occasional buyers. This data story uses AI-driven analysis from the retail chain BI dashboard to answer one strategic question: what behaviours and demographics most strongly predict a customer's lifetime value — and what should the business do about it?

The data story
01

The single most powerful loyalty driver is purchase frequency. Power BI's Key Influencers AI visual revealed that when a customer's purchase frequency increases by just 8.71 visits, they become 7.55× more likely to be classified as VIP. No other variable came close to this effect size — not spending amount, not age, not location.

02

Young customers are a high-potential loyalty segment. Customers aged 18–24 are 1.37× more likely to become VIP when frequency is high — suggesting that early loyalty programme engagement with younger shoppers yields disproportionate long-term returns.

03

Geography quietly shapes occasional behaviour. Customers from Selangor are 1.44× more likely to be classified as Occasional buyers — a signal worth investigating. Is this a store accessibility issue, a competitive market, or a gap in the loyalty programme's regional reach?

04

Gender plays a subtle role in regular-tier retention. Female customers are 1.23× more likely to fall into the Regular segment — indicating that loyalty programme messaging and incentives may benefit from gender-aware personalisation to push this group toward VIP.

Strategic recommendations
🎯 Frequency is the lever — not spend.
Design loyalty rewards around visit frequency (e.g. visit-based stamps, monthly return bonuses) rather than minimum spend thresholds.
🧑‍🎓 Young customers are future VIPs.
Launch targeted onboarding campaigns for 18–24 age group at first purchase — convert early habitual shoppers before competitors do.
📍 Selangor shows weaker loyalty signals.
Investigate store accessibility, local competition, and whether loyalty programme promotions adequately reach Selangor-based customers.
Why this matters

This analysis demonstrates the core value of Business Intelligence: moving beyond descriptive reporting — "here is what happened" — toward prescriptive insight — "here is what you should do next, and why." The AI visual did not replace human judgement; it gave management a data-backed foundation on which to build smarter, faster decisions about where to invest in customer relationships.
